legal_basis: Loi du 18 decembre 2015 relative a la NCD (Norme commune de declaration / CRS); transposant la Directive EU 2014/107/UE (DAC2)
crs_status: Committed and active - first exchange 2017 (early adopter). Key fund domicile.
reporting_deadline: 30 June following the 31 December reporting year-end
submission_method: MANDATORY via accredited intermediary only: FUNDSQUARE (Luxembourg Stock Exchange subsidiary) or WORLDLINE. Direct submission to ACD is NOT available. FIs must contract with one of these intermediaries.
xml_schema: OECD CRS XML Schema v2.0; intermediary platforms apply additional validation
nil_report_required: Yes - mandatory from 2021 onwards. Nil CRS report must be submitted even if zero reportable accounts.
msg_ref_id_format: LU + FI registration number (RCS or CSSF number) + year + sequence
doc_ref_id_format: LU + year + FI registration number + unique account reference
local_fi_scope: Fonds d'investissement (investment funds): UCITS, AIF - Luxembourg is major fund domicile
Societes de gestion (management companies) of UCITS and AIFs
Fiducies and trusts registered in Luxembourg
Luxembourg-domiciled special purpose vehicles (SPVs) classified as Investment Entities
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
Pension savings contracts (regime complementaire de retraite) - specifically excluded under Luxembourg law
local_specific_rules: MANDATORY INTERMEDIARY SUBMISSION: Luxembourg does not operate a direct FI-to-ACD portal. ALL submissions must go through FUNDSQUARE or WORLDLINE. FIs must have a service agreement with one of these platforms. This is a Luxembourg-specific structural requirement with no OECD equivalent.

FUNDSQUARE DOMINANCE: The vast majority of Luxembourg fund industry CRS submissions go through FUNDSQUARE, a subsidiary of the Luxembourg Stock Exchange (Bourse de Luxembourg). WORLDLINE is the alternative for non-fund FIs.

NIL REPORTS MANDATORY: From 2021, Luxembourg requires a nil CRS report to be submitted for every year in which a Reporting FI has zero reportable accounts. Earlier years (pre-2021) did not require nil reports.

FUND INDUSTRY SCALE: Luxembourg is the second-largest fund domicile globally. A single fund manager may have hundreds of sub-funds each classified as separate Reporting FIs. FI registration and intermediary coordination is therefore complex.
EU DAC2 OVERLAY: As an EU member, Luxembourg applies DAC2 for exchanges with EU partners. MCAA applies for non-EU partners.
NCD TERMINOLOGY: Luxembourg uses the French term 'Norme commune de declaration (NCD)' as the local equivalent of CRS. All local regulations and ACD guidance use this term.
CSSF SUPERVISION: The Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF) is the prudential regulator for Luxembourg banks and funds. Compliance with NCD/CRS is supervised by both CSSF and ACD.
SUB-FUND REPORTING: Under Luxembourg UCITS/AIF law, each sub-fund of an umbrella fund is treated as a separate legal entity for CRS purposes and must be registered and reported separately.
FATCA COORDINATION: Luxembourg's FATCA reporting also flows through the intermediary (FUNDSQUARE/WORLDLINE) to ACD, which passes to IRS under the Luxembourg-US Model 1 IGA.

rejection_error_codes.resolution: Validate against OECD CRS XSD; check FUNDSQUARE technical specifications

rejection_error_codes.desc: FI not registered with ACD as Reporting FI
rejection_error_codes.resolution: Register FI with ACD before first submission via FUNDSQUARE portal
Use OECD2 correction record with CorrDocRefId
Contact ACD for extension; document cause for delay

penalties.failure_to_report: Under Luxembourg NCD law: fines up to EUR 250,000 per year for non-compliance
penalties.late_submission: ACD may impose administrative penalty; first occurrence typically managed with written warning
penalties.failure_to_register: Regulatory action by ACD and CSSF

fatca.portal: Via FUNDSQUARE or WORLDLINE (same as CRS)
key_rules: Luxembourg Model 1A: Luxembourg FIs report FATCA data to ACD via FUNDSQUARE/WORLDLINE; ACD passes to IRS.
US indicia: standard 7 indicia. Luxembourg funds must apply indicia review to all account holders.
FATCA submission also mandatory via accredited intermediary.

sources.notes: ACD CRS/NCD page - fetched and verified

gaps: FUNDSQUARE technical specification version should be confirmed
ACD penalty amounts should be verified against current Luxembourg NCD law text
